title: allium crispum
intro: Allium crispum, also known as walking onion or top-setting onion, is a type of perennial onion plant characterized by its unique growth habit of producing offsets on the tips of its leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Allium crispum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
